title: Pearson Edexcel International GCSE Biology Paper 1B - June/November 2020
subject: Biology

sub_questions.ms_text:
C cytoplasm

A is not correct as cellulose is not found in the bacterium
B is not correct as chitin is not found in the bacterium
D is not correct as a nucleus is not found in the bacterium

C 7

A is not correct because 1 is not neutral pH
B is not correct because 2 is not neutral pH
D is not correct because 12 is not neutral pH

An explanation that makes reference the following points:
• mutation (1)
• variation (1)
• survive (1)
• reproduce / breed / offspring (1)
• pass on allele / gene (1)

An answer that makes reference the following points:
• probiotic / cranberry / both / treatments (better than control) reduce (bacteria) /eq(1)
• more reduction if taken together / eq (1)
• cranberry (alone) reduces more than probiotic (alone) / eq(1)

• sexual (1)
• pollen (1)
• anther (1)
• stigma (1)
• bees / moths / flies (1)
• large / big / scented / (sweet) smelling (1)
• style (1)
• fertilisation (1)
• nucleus

convert length into µm (60 mm = 60 000 µm) (1)
division (60 000 ÷ 6 = ×10 000) (1)
award full marks for correct numerical answer without working
1 mark for 60 000 or dividing by 6

A 0.33

B is not correct as 3 is not the mean
C is not correct as 10 000 is not the mean
D is not correct as 75 000 000 is not the mean

An answer that makes reference to the following (allow converse for egg):
• sperm smaller / sperm is small cell / eq (1) [larger]
• fewer (total) mitochondria (per cell) (1) [more]
• more mitochondria per volume / per µm3 (1) [fewer]
• uses energy to swim / move / get to /eq (1) [does not move]
• fertilise egg (1) [egg is fertilised]

C primary consumer

A is not correct as krill is not a predator
B is not correct as prey is not a trophic level
D is not correct as krill is not a secondary consumer

A sketch that shows the following points:
• upright pyramid shape (1) [ignore names]
• names in correct order: plants at bottom, krill in middle, whale at top (1) [ignore shape]

division (10 000 ÷ 1.6 = 6 250 s) (1)
division (÷ 60 = 104.17 / 100 / 104 / 104.16 recurring)
award full marks for correct numerical answer without working
allow 100 mins as 2 sig figs
allow 1 mark for dividing by 1.6 or dividing by 96
or 1.6 x 60 = 96 cm2 per minute
10 000 ÷ 96 = 104.17 / 100 / 104 / 104.2 / / 104.167 / 104.16 recurring

An answer that makes reference to the following points:
• use container of sea water (1)
• add same / stated number/ amount/ count number / mass of microscopic plants (1)
• add stated number / mass of krill (1)
• leave for same / stated time / measure time taken to (1)
• (re)measure number / amount/mass / percentage change in plants(1)
• repeat /eq (1)

An explanation that makes reference the four of the following points:
• fewer whales / whales die / migrate (1)
• ice melts (1)
• no / less surface for microscopic plants (1)
• so fewer microscopic plants (available) (1)
• fewer krill / krill die / migrate (1)
• carbon dioxide causes acidification (1)
• could affect krill (eggs) (1)

A description that makes reference to three of the following points:
• (same) restriction enzyme to cut / remove / open /eq (1)
• plasmid / vector and target gene / gene for making poison / eq (1)
• ligase enzyme to join / stick / insert /eq (1)
• complementary shapes / sticky ends /eq (1)

An answer that makes reference to six of the following points:

Good decision to use GM:
• GM plants are specific / kill specific insects / pesticides kill other insects / plants / pesticide kills non-specific insects (1)
• GM plants reduce need for pesticide (1)
• pesticide needs reapplication / do not last long (1)
• insects can become resistant to pesticide (1)
• pesticides can enter food chains / bioaccumulation (1) [ignore harmful unqualified pollution, allow poisons humans]
• pesticides can lead to health problems / affect human health /eq (1) eg: organophosphates and nervous system

Poor decision to use GM:
• pesticides are quick to kill (1)
• GM plants could result in cross pollination of other species (1)
• GM pollen may kill insect pollinators (1)
• insects may develop resistance to poison produced by GM crops (1)
• customers may not buy GM crops (1)
• patents mean farmers become dependent on companies for their seed (1)

D C6H12O6 + 6O2 -> 6CO2 + 6H2O

A is not the correct equation for aerobic respiration
B is not the correct equation for aerobic respiration
C is not the correct equation for aerobic respiration

An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• moves left / towards locust (1)
• carbon dioxide produced by insect absorbed by KOH / filter paper (1)
• oxygen (used by insect) (1) [ignore air]
• in respiration (1) [ignore breathing /inhaling/eq]

An answer that makes reference to six of the following points:
• size / mass /(of locusts )/eq (1) [mark 3 variables to maximise score]
• (more cells) (more) respiration (1)
or
• movement / size of flask /eq (1)
• more room for movement means more respiration (1)
or
• volume / concentration of KOH / size of filter paper (1) [ignore amount but allow stated volume]
• affects carbon dioxide absorption /eq (1)
or
• temperature (1)
• affects enzymes / increase kinetic energy / particle movement /eq (1)
or
• time / duration (1) [ignore time of day / light / oxygen / humidity etc]
• more time to respire / more oxygen absorbed (1)
• 5.3 or 5.27 or 5.267 or 5.26 recurring

An answer that makes reference to the following points:
• not reliable / reliability can be increased /eq (1)
• not enough repeats / only three results / (1) [ignore not repeated]
• one result is anomalous / male 3 result is anomalous (1)
• more variation within sex than between sexes / eq (1)

A description that makes reference to two the following:
• biuret (1)
• deepest / darker / intensity purple contains most protein (1)
• reference to same volume of milk / biuret (1)
[allow other quantitative methods such as solidify the milk protein by denaturing (1), mass of the protein (1)]

An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• prevent infection / disease (1) [ignore illness]
• (by destroying / due to) virus / bacteria / pathogen (1)
• provides immunity / eq (1)

An answer that includes two of:
• use as a source of / for energy / respiration (1)
• use as store of energy (1)
• insulation / myelin sheath / protection fat around organs/ eq(1) [ignore for warmth]
lactose
Lactobacillus / Streptococcus [Allow species names]

An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• sterilise milk / pasteurise (1)
• kill bacteria / pathogen / microorganisms (1)
• prevent competition (for carbohydrate / sugar) (1)

D testis

A is incorrect as meiosis does not occur in kidney
B is incorrect as meiosis does not occur in penis
C is incorrect as meiosis does not occur in skin

C root tip

A is incorrect as mitosis is not observed in anther
B is incorrect as mitosis is not observed in cotyledon
D is incorrect as mitosis is not observed in xylem

number of chromosomes in each original cell: Meiosis 46, Mitosis 46
number of daughter cells produced from each original cell: Meiosis 4, Mitosis 2
number of chromosomes in each daughter cell: Meiosis 23, Mitosis 46
ploidy level of daughter cells produced: Meiosis haploid, Mitosis diploid
genetic differences in daughter cells: Meiosis present, Mitosis absent /none/identical /eq
type of cell produced: Meiosis gamete / sperm / egg / sex cell, Mitosis body cell
[1 mark for each row]

A description that makes reference to the following:
• random mating (1)
• random fertilisation / gametes received/ eq (1)
• environment (1)
• mutation (1)

An explanation to the following points:
• little / no genetic variation / have same genotype / alleles (1)
• no genotype environment interaction / respond to drugs in same way /eq (1)

An answer that includes:
• S scale linear and at least half axis (1)
• L straight lines through points (1) [bar chart lose L only, No L for extrapolation]
• A axis labelled with crop production and year and correct way round (1)
• P points accurately within one small square plotted (1)
• U units thousand tonnes (and years) (1)
• K labelled or key to show barley and wheat (1)

A description that makes reference to the following:
• wheat decreases to 2013/ eq and then increases (1)
• barley constant and then increases to 2013 / eq (then fluctuates) (1)

percentage change in wheat = (16 100 – 15 300) ÷ 15 300 × 100 = 5.23% allow 5.2%/ 5%/eq (1) [allow 105% /eq]
percentage change in barley = (7 300 – 5 500) ÷ 5 500 × 100 = 32.73% allow 32.7/ 33% [allow 133% /eq]
barley greater (1)
allow one mark for greater change in barley
allow one mark for each correct percentage

25 000 kg per 10 000 m2
25 000 ÷ 10 000 = 2.5 kg per m2 per year
2.5 kg = 2500 g ÷ 365 = 6.85 g [allow 6.9]
also allow for leap year 2500 / 365.25 so 6.84 g [allow 6.8g]
allow full credit for correct answer no working
one mark for dividing by 365 or 365.25

• A sensory / afferent (1)
• B relay / association (1)
• C motor / efferent (1)

An explanation that makes reference to the following points:
• neurone A / sensory / afferent (+ impulse) (from receptor) to CNS / spinal cord / relay neurone / neurone B (1) [must have impulse once to score 3 marks, allow signal message for 2 max]
• (synapses with) neurone B / relay / association / (+ impulse) (from receptor neurone A) to motor / efferent / neurone C
• neurone C / motor / efferent (+ impulse) to effector / muscle

An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• measure the distance (from hand to spinal cord and or brain to other hand) (1) [ignore count number of students]
• for each student / all students in ring (1)
• measure time taken (for student A to feel hand being squeezed / eq) (1) [allow ‘how long it took’]
• divide distance by time (1)

An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points:
• may be underestimate / too slow / method adds time / delay / eq (1)
• not a reflex so need to allow decision making / role of brain / reaction time /eq (1)
• as delay occurs at each synapse (1)

An answer that makes reference to six of the following points:
• C shampoo with oil and without oil / shampoo with oil and no shampoo (1)
• O hair from same person / same hair length / width / age / sex/ same hair type / dry hair same way (1)
• R test many different hairs / repeat (1)
• M1 add weights / masses to hairs (1)
• M2 measure mass / g / pressure / weight / force /N that causes hair to break (1)
• S1 same volume / concentration of shampoo / same type of shampoo with and without oil (1) [ignore amount]
• S2 wash for same time / frequency / same temperature of water (1)
